properties.name: Sedona
properties.description: Famous for its red rock scenery, Sedona was one of the first designated Dark Sky Communities in the US.

properties.name: Flagstaff
properties.description: The world's first International Dark Sky City, home to Lowell Observatory.

properties.name: Beverly Shores
properties.description: Nestled within Indiana Dunes National Park on the southern shore of Lake Michigan.

properties.name: Old Snowmass
properties.description: A rural community in the Roaring Fork Valley surrounded by the Elk Mountains.

properties.name: Big Bend National Park
properties.description: One of the least light-polluted places in the lower 48 states, offering stunning Milky Way views.

properties.name: Arches National Park
properties.description: Known for its dramatic red rock landscape and over 2,000 natural stone arches.

properties.name: Death Valley National Park
properties.description: The largest dark sky park in the US, offering some of the darkest skies in North America.

properties.name: Glacier National Park
properties.description: A stunning wilderness area offering incredible views of the Milky Way over glacial lakes.

properties.name: Greater Big Bend
properties.description: The largest International Dark Sky Reserve in the world, covering over 54,000 km².

properties.name: Central Idaho
properties.description: The first Dark Sky Reserve in the US, covering 1,416 square miles of protected dark skies.

properties.name: Cosmic Campground
properties.description: The first International Dark Sky Sanctuary in the Northern Hemisphere.

properties.name: Astronomy Village
properties.description: A private community dedicated to astronomy and dark sky preservation in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
